name: Chozas de Abajo from Oncina de la Valdoncina via the Camino Francés
name_original: Chozas de Abajo desde Oncina de la Valdoncina por el Camino Francés
region_intro: Hiking around Valverde De La Virgen, located in the province of León, Spain, offers diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is a significant point on the Camino de Santiago (French Way), featuring accessible trails and natural beauty. Landscapes include the heathland of the Páramo and areas where the Raposeras and Oncina streams converge. Cultural landmarks like the Santuario de la Virgen del Camino also add to the area's appeal. Best hiking trails around Valverde De La Virgen The most popular hiking route is Chozas de Abajo from Oncina de la Valdoncina via the Camino Francés, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino Francés. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de Fuente Blanca by Chozas de Abajo and Villar de Mazarife — by circular by the camino francés, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This circular route passes through local villages and offers varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Bridge – Imprint loop from Trobajo del Camino, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Valverde De La Virgen is defined by its connection to the Camino de Santiago, the open heathland of the Páramo, and the confluence of local streams. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging routes. The routes in Valverde De La Virgen are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Valverde De La Virgen's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: Valverde De La Virgen offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 170 trails documented. These include 85 easy routes, 84 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The majority of hikes in Valverde De La Virgen are classified as easy or moderate. There are 85 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, and 84 moderate routes for those looking for a bit more challenge. A few difficult trails are also available for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hikes around Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The terrain around Valverde De La Virgen is quite diverse. You'll encounter sections of the historic Camino de Santiago, open heathland known as the Páramo, and areas where the Raposeras and Oncina streams converge. This mix offers varied scenery, from cultural pathways to natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular hiking routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169326770/laguna-de-fuente-blanca-by-chozas-de-abajo-and-villar-de-mazarife-by-circular-by-the-camino-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Fuente Blanca by Chozas de Abajo and Villar de Mazarife — by circular by the camino francés&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path that takes you through local villages and varied scenery.

Q: What cultural or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore significant landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt; is a notable architectural site. Other points of interest include the Santuario de la Virgen del Camino, a significant pilgrimage center, and the Parish Church of Santa Engracia, known for its ancient bell tower and nesting storks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The trails in Valverde De La Virgen are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's connection to the Camino de Santiago and the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: Yes, with 85 easy routes, Valverde De La Virgen offers many options suitable for families. These trails generally feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: Many trails in the Valverde De La Virgen area, especially those along the Camino de Santiago, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near villages, and to carry water for them.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is typically available in the villages and towns that serve as trailheads, such as Oncina de la Valdoncina or Trobajo del Camino. As many routes are part of the Camino de Santiago, these areas are generally accustomed to accommodating visitors.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads?
A: Given Valverde De La Virgen's location on the Camino de Santiago, public transport options, such as local buses, often connect the main towns and villages. These can be useful for reaching various starting points or for returning after a linear hike.

Q: Are there any easy walking routes suitable for beginners?
A: Absolutely. Valverde De La Virgen boasts 85 easy routes, perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. These trails typically have minimal elevation gain and are well-suited for a leisurely exploration of the local scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best times for hiking in Valverde De La Virgen. The weather is typically mild and pleasant, ideal for enjoying the trails and the natural beauty of the region, including the nesting storks in the church towers.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in Valverde De La Virgen?
A: No specific permits are generally required for hiking on the public trails around Valverde De La Virgen, including sections of the Camino de Santiago. The trails are freely accessible for hikers.
tips: Nice views of the agricultural fields in this section of the Camino Frances between Oncina de la Valdoncina and Chozas de Abajo.

The Church of Chozas de Abajo is located in the central square of the town, next to the House of Culture. It is a modern and simple construction, with a bell tower that stands out for its artistic design and breaks with traditional rules. This church is an important landmark in the town and is part of local festivities, such as the Fiestas de San Martín on November 11 and the Fiesta del Verano on August 10.

waypoints.name: View of the agricultural fields along the Camino Francés between Oncina de la Valdoncina and Chozas de Abajo
